items.criterion: Edge function returns a `message` string field when LLM includes a // MESSAGE: line in code generation responses
Edge function returns `isQuestionResponse: true` with message and no code when user input is a question
Question-only responses do not trigger code validation errors or retry logic
EffectCreatorPanel displays the agent message inline when present
Agent message is dismissible and clears on new generation or form reset
Question-only responses do not overwrite existing code/name/schema state in the panel
Existing effect generation flow (create, edit, retry) continues to work unchanged when no MESSAGE line is present
Message display is visually distinct but not intrusive — subtle styling consistent with the panel's existing design language
LLM reliably distinguishes questions from effect requests (>90% accuracy on obvious cases)
No new files created — all changes are edits to existing files
Edge function response size does not increase significantly for normal code generation (message is 1-2 sentences)
Manual smoke tests: generate effect with message, ask a question, edit existing effect with message, dismiss message, verify retry still works

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect generation triage: The single-call approach is under-specified against the current prompt contract, which is still strongly code-generation-only. In `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts` the system rules require `// NAME`, `// DESCRIPTION`, `// PARAMS`, `React.createElement`, and `exports.default` ([lines 60-74](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts), [lines 126-129](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts)), while the user message still says `Build a ... Remotion effect` and edit mode says to `modify ONLY what the user asked for` ([lines 155-166](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts), [lines 183-188](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts)). Adding a conversational exception without rewriting that framing is likely to make question-only responses unreliable, especially in edit mode.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The repository's existing prompt builder hard-frames every request as effect creation/editing, so the proposed 'question handling' addition conflicts with the rest of the prompt instead of clearly overriding it.

unresolved_flags.concern: Effect generation parsing: The proposed classifier `no exports.default + MESSAGE => question response` is unsafe and would bypass the repo's existing retry-on-invalid-code path. Today `extractEffectCodeAndMeta()` always validates extracted code and throws on missing `exports.default`, which `index.ts` catches to self-invoke a retry ([`templates.ts` lines 365-380](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts), [`templates.ts` lines 402-420](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/templates.ts), [`index.ts` lines 301-339](supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.ts)). If `// MESSAGE:` becomes part of normal successful generations too, a malformed code response that includes a message but forgets the default export would be misclassified as a successful conversational reply instead of retried.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan's heuristic collapses 'question answer' and 'broken generation output' into the same shape, but the current implementation depends on validation failures throwing so retries happen.

rationale: Both blocking flags identify real correctness risks that the current plan doesn't adequately address. Flag 1: The plan adds a small conversational exception to OUTPUT_RULES but leaves the rest of the prompt (system message, user message templates) strongly framing every request as code generation. An LLM receiving "Build a ... Remotion effect" in the user message with a small exception clause buried in output rules will unreliably detect questions. The plan needs to restructure the prompt more carefully — likely by moving question detection to the system message level and conditionally changing the user message template when no existing code is present. Flag 2: The `no exports.default + MESSAGE => question` heuristic is genuinely unsafe. A malformed code generation (LLM includes MESSAGE but forgets the default export) would silently succeed as a "question response" instead of triggering the retry path. The plan needs a more robust discriminator — e.g., a distinct `// QUESTION_RESPONSE: true` marker that the LLM only outputs for actual questions, separate from `// MESSAGE:` which appears on all responses. This way, malformed code with a MESSAGE line but no QUESTION_RESPONSE marker still hits validation and gets retried.
signals_assessment: Iteration 1 with a weighted score of 4.0. Two significant flags remain open, both in the correctness category, targeting the core triage/parsing logic that is central to the plan's design. No flags have been resolved yet and no prior iterations exist. Preflight checks all pass. The flags are well-evidenced against specific code locations and represent likely runtime failures, not theoretical concerns.
warnings: The prompt restructuring for question detection should be tested against edit-mode prompts specifically — edit mode has even stronger code-generation framing than create mode.
Consider whether question detection should be disabled entirely in edit mode (where code already exists) to avoid the complexity of supporting it there in v1.

settled_decisions.decision: Single LLM call (no separate triage classifier) is the right latency tradeoff — but the prompt must clearly partition question vs code responses at the system message level, not as a footnote in output rules.
settled_decisions.rationale: A separate triage call adds 2-5s latency for every request. The single-call approach is fine if the prompt is structured to make question detection reliable.

settled_decisions.decision: Inline message box (not toast) is the correct UI pattern for agent messages.
settled_decisions.rationale: Toasts auto-dismiss and are disconnected from the interaction context. An inline dismissible box near the prompt area keeps the message in context and lets the user reference it while composing their next prompt.

settled_decisions.decision: MESSAGE metadata line on code responses is the right approach for agent commentary — just needs to be decoupled from question detection.
settled_decisions.rationale: Adding a // MESSAGE: line to successful code generations is low-risk and useful. The issue is only when MESSAGE is used as part of the question-detection heuristic.

orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: ITERATE. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Add agent response messages to the effect generation flow. Three things: (1) When the ai-generate-effect edge function completes (success or retry-success), the response should include an optional 'message' field — a user-facing message from the LLM explaining what it built, any creative choices it made, or acknowledging the user's request. The LLM prompt should ask for this. (2) The user's effect creation prompt input should also support questions/conversation — if the user asks a question instead of requesting an effect, the edge function should detect this and return a conversational response (message only, no code) instead of generating code. This means the edge function needs a triage step. (3) On the frontend (EffectCreatorPanel), show the agent's message in an ephemeral toast/box near the effect creator. Since the flow is synchronous (user waits for response), show the message when the response arrives and let it persist until dismissed or the user starts a new generation. Think about: where in the UI this message appears, how it interacts with the existing effect preview, whether it should be a toast vs inline message vs expandable panel, and how question-only responses (no code) should be displayed differently from effect-generated responses.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 4.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.
